senses.definition: plural of quincunx: a pattern or arrangement of five points or objects in a cross-like or X-shaped form, with one at each corner and one in the center.
examples: The die shows a quincunx arrangement when the five spots form a cross pattern.
Ancient coins were often stamped with a quincunx emblem.
The garden beds were laid out in a quincunx for symmetry.

senses.definition: relating to or shaped like a quincunx (five points arranged with four at the corners and one in the center).
examples: The necklace featured a quincunxial pattern of gems.
A quincunxial arrangement was used in the garden design.

senses.definition: a male given name; short form of the name Francis or Quincy; also historically a town name.
examples: Quincy bought a gift for his sister.
The town of Quincy hosts an annual festival.

senses.definition: a colloquial or historical term for the medical condition 'quinsy' (strept throat), used in some dialects.
examples: He was diagnosed with quinsy in the Middle Ages.

senses.definition: a historical or rare term for the number fourteen; grecized form sometimes used in numerology or heraldry.
examples: The tablet bore a quindecad mark indicating fourteen items.

senses.definition: a polygon with fourteen sides.
examples: A quindecagon has fourteen equal sides in a regular form.
The architect designed a quindecagon floor plan.

senses.definition: plural of quindecagon; multiple fourteen-sided polygons.
examples: The tessellation used several quindecagons interlinked with other polygons.

senses.definition: a shape with fourteen angles; often used to describe a fourteen-angled polygon.
examples: The map included a quindecangle boundary.

senses.definition: an element or component consisting of fourteen units; a rare term in mathematical notation.
examples: The symbol represented a quindecaplet in the diagram.

senses.definition: plural of quindecaplet; multiple fourteen-unit components.
examples: The model used several quindecaplets to illustrate the concept.

senses.definition: having fourteen syllables.
examples: The line of poetry was quindecasyllabic but difficult to recite.
A quindecasyllabic verse often appears in classical prosody.

senses.definition: a member of a group of fourteen men in ancient Rome who held certain religious or civic duties.
examples: The quindecemviri were responsible for important rites.

senses.definition: the group or body of fourteen men (the quindecemviri) collectively.
examples: The quindecemvirate oversaw the rites.

senses.definition: plural form referring to the fourteen men who held certain offices in ancient Rome.
examples: The quindecemviri advised the magistrates.

senses.definition: occurring every fourteen years; relating to a fourteen-year period.
examples: The festival is held on a quindecennial basis.

senses.definition: a quindecennial period or anniversary.
examples: They marked the quindecennial of the town's founding.

senses.definition: plural of quindecennial; multiple fourteen-year periods or anniversaries.
examples: The village celebrated its quindecennials with a parade.

senses.definition: the number 1 followed by 48 zeros in short scale (or 1 followed by 66 zeros in long scale, depending on usage); a very large number used in mathematics or informal speech.
examples: In the joke, there were quindecillions of stars.

senses.definition: plural of quindecillion; multiple extremely large numbers.
examples: The textbook describes quindecillions in a joke about numbers.

senses.definition: the ordinal form of quindecillion; the position after the quindecillionth item in a sequence of extremely large values.
examples: The fraction is one quindecillionth of a whole.

senses.definition: the number fourteen in Latin; used in historical or scholarly contexts.
examples: The term appears in Latin inscriptions as quindecim.

senses.definition: the feminine form of the Latin number fourteen; used in some historical or scholarly texts.
examples: The inscription used quindecima to denote the year.

senses.definition: a member of a group of fourteen men in ancient Rome who held specific duties.
examples: The quindecimvir oversaw religious rites.

senses.definition: relating to or containing fourteen carbon atoms or a fourteen-carbon structure in chemistry.
examples: Quindecylic acid is a chemical compound with fourteen carbon atoms.

senses.definition: a hydrocarbon with a specific bicyclic structure, part of the naphthalene family.
examples: Quindene is used in some chemical synthesis pathways.

senses.definition: a short test in computer science to verify a hypothesis; also a type of clever puzzle or a truncated form of 'quince' in some contexts; the term is also used in linguistics to denote a specific linguistic construction.
examples: The coder ran a quick quine to test the simulator.

senses.definition: a small (often decorative) pendant or small coin or gambling term; historically used in some Spanish-speaking regions.
examples: The old pendant bore a quine la motif.
